reason: Primary architectural boundary for terminal performance, resize, focus, and SwiftUI/AppKit ownership.
Native UI component, AppKit bridge, document surface, file manager, and shell interaction contracts.
State-frequency boundaries and forbidden high-frequency SwiftUI state patterns.
Animation and motion contracts for perceived responsiveness and hand feel.
Frontend quality and verification expectations for UI performance and polish.
Ghostty/libghostty hosting contracts relevant to focus, geometry, rendering, and PTY behavior.
Runtime performance, threading, persistence, and background-work quality rules.
Diagnostics and instrumentation expectations for performance investigation.
P1 interaction fix: cache file-manager display snapshots so body, search bar, status bar, and row rendering reuse one filtered/flattened row set instead of recomputing on every subview access.
P1 interaction fix: mount only the selected workspace file editor; removed hidden inactive editor surfaces from layout and resize work.
P1 interaction fix: keep the document WKWebView identity stable across layout revision updates and cache bundled vendor script/style/base64 resources.
Follow-up P1 fix: moved file-row detail/binary/large-file checks out of row body into directory loading, removed synchronous fileExists checks and per-row hover state from scroll/mouse-move path.
Regression fix: restored workspace file search visibility for source/code/log/Markdown-source modes while keeping Markdown preview/split on the document search bridge.
Focus regression fix: paused source editor first-responder requests while the workspace search field is visible so the search input keeps focus instead of flashing and losing the caret.
Terminal search focus fix: navigation and split refresh paths no longer restore first responder to the terminal host while terminal search is visible.

Terminal search focus guard: ConductorWindow now rejects TerminalHostView first-responder requests while terminal search is visible, preventing any remaining host focus path from stealing the search input.
Search focus stability: context search fields now confirm focus across multiple main-thread turns after mounting, covering view insertion and animation timing.
Search input focus fix: NSTextField now explicitly enters AppKit field-editor editing mode when focused, so the caret lands inside the input instead of only focusing the control wrapper.

Search focus retention fix: while terminal search is visible, ConductorWindow allows only the active search field or its field editor to keep first responder, blocking later responder changes that make the caret flash and disappear.
Search focus retention fix: focused search fields register themselves with the ConductorWindow so the window-level responder guard can preserve the active field editor.

Search component rewrite: replaced the custom NSTextField focus wrapper with a native NSSearchField bridge that owns search editing, live updates, Return/arrow navigation, and Escape handling locally.
Search component cleanup: removed the over-broad window-level active search field responder guard after moving focus ownership into the native NSSearchField component.

Root-cause terminal search focus fix: stopped opening Ghostty's native start_search prompt when showing the Conductor search bar, so Ghostty no longer competes for keyboard focus with the app-owned search field.
Terminal search focus race fix: search field focus now verifies the AppKit field editor across several post-mount frames instead of treating a single early makeFirstResponder call as sufficient.
Terminal search focus source fix: ConductorWindow now rejects TerminalHostView first-responder requests while terminal search is visible instead of relying on search-field focus retries.
Removed search-field focus retry loop after moving focus suppression to the window responder boundary.
